# Concepts

- [Credit for taxes paid to another municipality](credit-for-taxes-paid.md) - How the credit factor and credit limit work, and why the same WFH refund nets real money in Medina but roughly zero in Brecksville.
- [Ohio municipal income tax basics](municipal-income-tax-basics.md) - What Ohio municipal income tax is, who levies it, what RITA is, and the two hooks - where you live and where you work - that decide who taxes your wages.
- [This bundle briefs, it does not advise](not-tax-advice.md) - How a consuming agent should use this bundle - brief the person with cited facts, flag year-sensitivity, and route final decisions to RITA or a preparer.
- [Open claim windows (living table, verified 2026-07-14)](open-claim-windows.md) - Which tax years are still claimable on Form 10A right now - as of July 2026, TY2023, 2024, and 2025 are open; 2020-2022 are closed (2022 extension filers until ~Oct 2026).
- [Residence tax vs. workplace tax](residence-vs-workplace-tax.md) - The two overlapping municipal taxes on the same paycheck - the workplace city taxes wages earned there, the residence city taxes everything with a partial or full credit.
- [RITA vs. CCA vs. self-administered cities](rita-vs-cca-vs-self-administered.md) - Three kinds of tax administration in NE Ohio - RITA members, CCA members (notably Cleveland), and cities that run their own tax departments (Brunswick, Parma, Lakewood, Fairlawn). Form 10A is RITA-only.
- [Statute of limitations for municipal tax refunds](statute-of-limitations.md) - ORC 718.19(B)(1) - refund requests must be filed within three years after the return (including any valid extension) was due or paid, whichever is later.
- [Townships - no municipal income tax (usually)](townships-and-unincorporated.md) - Ohio townships cannot levy income tax, so township residents owe no residence municipal tax - the JEDD/JEDZ exception and why "I live in a township" changes the whole refund math.
- [The 20-day occasional entrant rule](twenty-day-occasional-entrant.md) - ORC 718.011 - no withholding for a city where an employee worked 20 or fewer days that year, with principal-place-of-work, presumed-worksite, and residence exceptions.
- [Work-from-home rules by tax year](work-from-home-rules-by-year.md) - The hub node - three legal regimes for remote-work days (2020 under HB 197, 2021 under HB 110, 2022+ under ordinary ORC 718) and what each means for refunds.
